model_narrative: We plan delivery routes for a fleet of vehicles based at a single depot that together must serve a set of customers, each with a known demand. For every vehicle we decide which direct trips between locations it makes, and we track the position of each location along the route that serves it. Every vehicle has a limited carrying capacity. The objective is to minimize the total distance travelled by the entire fleet.

sets.name: node
sets.doc: all locations, comprising the single depot and the customer nodes that must be served

sets.name: vehicle
sets.doc: the vehicles in the fleet, each based at the depot

sets.name: depot
sets.doc: the depot node where every vehicle starts and ends its route; it is a subset of the node set and the remaining nodes are the customers

sets.name: arc
sets.members: all triples (i, j, k) where i and j are distinct nodes and k is a vehicle
sets.doc: the allowed directed travel options: vehicle k may drive directly from node i to node j whenever i and j are different nodes

params.name: demand
params.doc: the amount of goods required by the customer at each node, in load units; the depot has demand zero

params.name: capacity
params.doc: the maximum total load each vehicle can carry, in load units

params.name: distance
params.doc: the travel distance between each ordered pair of nodes, in distance units

params.name: card
params.doc: the number of nodes in the problem; used as the large constant in the ordering inequalities, with value equal to the node count

vars.name: X
vars.doc: equals 1 if vehicle k travels directly from node i to node j, and 0 otherwise; defined only over allowed arcs

vars.name: P
vars.doc: a position variable for each node giving its order along the route that visits it, ranging between 0 and one less than the node count; it is pinned to 0 at the depot

vars.name: totdist
vars.doc: the total distance travelled by the whole fleet

constraints.name: X[i,
constraints.intent: For each vehicle and each location, the number of trips that vehicle makes into the location must equal the number of trips it makes out of that location, so whenever a vehicle arrives somewhere it also departs from there.

constraints.name: depot:
constraints.intent: Every customer must be visited exactly once. Counting arrivals from any other location and by any vehicle, each customer location is entered a single time. The depot is excluded from this requirement.

constraints.name: X[d,
constraints.intent: Each vehicle must leave the depot exactly once, travelling directly from the depot to one of the customer locations.

constraints.name: demand[j]
constraints.intent: For each vehicle, the total demand of all the customers it serves must not exceed that vehicle's carrying capacity. A customer counts toward a vehicle's load when that vehicle drives directly into it, and the depot does not contribute any demand.

constraints.name: card
constraints.intent: To prevent routes that loop among the customers without connecting back to the depot, impose an ordering along each route. For every pair of distinct locations, whenever some vehicle drives directly from the first to the second, the second location's position along the route must come after the first location's position. The depot serves as the reference point of the ordering, so trips returning to the depot are handled accordingly. When no vehicle makes the direct trip between the two locations, the ordering requirement is relaxed so that it does not bind.

constraints.name: totdist
constraints.intent: The total distance travelled by the fleet equals the sum, over every direct trip actually made by any vehicle, of the distance of that trip.

query: We're toying with a direct express run: have one of the trucks leave the depot and go straight to node n16 first. If we require the depot-to-n16 arc to be switched on for some vehicle, what total distance does the optimizer land on?
expected_pyomo: model.force_n1n16 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.X['n1', 'n16', k] for k in model.vehicle) >= 1)

query: Suppose a vehicle has to set off from the depot directly to node n9 to drop a priority package there first. With the depot-to-n9 arc forced on for one of the trucks, where does the total mileage settle?
expected_pyomo: model.force_n1n9 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.X['n1', 'n9', k] for k in model.vehicle) >= 1)

query: We're curious what happens if a truck is sent out of the depot straight to node n12 as its opening move. Forcing the depot-to-n12 arc on for some vehicle, what's the resulting total distance?

query: The plan sends a whole truck out to n13 and straight back, which looks wasteful — a half-empty dedicated run. Shouldn't we ban that direct depot-to-n13 hop and fold n13 into another truck's loop instead? What's the model seeing that makes the dedicated run worth it?
expected_pyomo: model.ban_n1n13 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.X['n1', 'n13', k] for k in model.vehicle) == 0)

query: Node n5 is fairly far out, so wouldn't it make sense to give it a dedicated head start — a truck leaving the depot straight to n5? Couldn't we just require the depot-to-n5 arc to be used? What's pulling the optimizer away from that?

query: I'd have thought node n3 deserves a direct line from the depot rather than being buried mid-route. Shouldn't a truck just run depot-to-n3 first? Why is the model routing around that?
expected_pyomo: model.force_n1n3 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.X['n1', 'n3', k] for k in model.vehicle) >= 1)
